observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to intensify efforts in pursuing collection by following up with debtors, issuing the appropriate demand letters, and conducting a thorough review of the Other Receivables account to identify and substantiate dormant receivables in support of the Municipality’s claim. Additionally, the Municipal Accountant should assess the collectability of long-outstanding receivables and recognize provisions for any deemed uncollectible amounts, as necessary. Should there be no other remedies for collection, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive request authority to write-off dormant receivables pursuant to COA Circular No. 2023-008, and other relevant COA Circulars regarding writing off of receivables, ensuring that all required documentation is attached to avoid further delays.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Prepare and record the adjusting entries necessary to correct the balances of the PPE account and Interest Expenses; and
Strictly adhere to the provisions of IPSAS 5 to ensure proper and correct recording of borrowing costs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct:

a.	all employees to strictly comply with the provisions of the GAA, particularly the requirement to maintain the minimum monthly Net Take-Home Pay (NTHP) of ₱5,000; and
b.	the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to cease the practice of accepting loan payments that are not processed through or reflected in the LGU’s payroll.

We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive, upon authorization of the Sangguniang Bayan, consider amending the existing Memoranda of Agreement with the lending institutions to include a provision that employee loans should be granted solely based on their monthly NTHP, and that the resulting amortization must not reduce the monthly NTHP below ₱5,000.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the LSB to submit the quarterly and annual SEF utilization reports to the DepEd CO, thru appropriate channels and furnish copies to the Regional Office of the DBM within the prescribed timelines.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer and other concerned offices to comply with Section 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 by submitting to the Auditor, at the beginning of each year, a comprehensive list of all ongoing and planned Programs, Projects, and Activities (PPAs). The list should include the required details such as project name, implementing office, description, contractor or supplier (if any), mode of procurement, funding source, approved budget, project duration, and location. Timely submission of this list will enable the Audit Team to conduct effective validation and monitoring, and allow for the early detection and communication of any deficiencies for appropriate and immediate management action.

We recommended that the Head of the Procuring Entity (HOPE) avoid signing documents that gives an impression of participating in any BAC function, including the approval of BAC Resolutions related to bid evaluation and post-qualification. The HOPE should act only upon the final recommendation of award, after the BAC has completed its mandate in the bidding process. Future procurement activities must strictly adhere to the delineated roles under R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR to promote transparency, accountability, and legality in the procurement process.

We recommended that The Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to: a) conduct a thorough investigation to identify the root cause of the discrepancies between the financial statements and the cashbook after which take appropriate action to correct the discrepancies in accordance with the applicable accounting standards and guidelines; and b) implement adequate monitoring and reconciliation procedures for all accounts, including the Cash Local Treasury account, to prevent future discrepancies and ensure the accuracy of financial reports.
observations.managementAction: The Management has conducted an initial investigation to identify the root cause to of discrepancies and already implemented adequate monitoring and reconciliation procedures for all accounts to prevent future discrepancies.
observations.reason: Review of the Notes to Financial Statements for CY 2023 revealed that the Cash-Local Treasury account still has an abnormal negative balance of ₱8,469.96 indicating that no adjustment was made yet to correct the negative balance. Discussed fully in Part II, Observation No. 4.1, pages 47-49

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to exert more effort, follow-up on the debtors and serve corresponding demand letters, and conduct a thorough investigation on the Other Receivables account in identifying details of the dormant receivables to establish the claim of the Municipality. Also, to assess the recoverability of the long outstanding receivables and make provision for uncollectible receivables, as necessary. Should there be no other remedies for collection, we recommend the LCE to request for authority to write-off dormant receivables pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: The Management is doing their best effort in pursuing the collection of the dormant receivables. The request for authority to write-off the Advances to Special Disbursing Officer was delayed due to the non-submission of the deceased AO’s death certificate. As for the dissolved NGOs, they are still trying to identify the previous officers to whom they will send the demand letters. Should the collection of these receivables be impossible, a proof of insolvency and other necessary documents shall be required to be submitted by the previous officers in order for the Local Chief Executive to duly request for authority to write-off said accounts.
observations.reason: For review of the Notes to Financial Statements for CY 2023, the receivables are still outstanding as at December 31, 2023 signifying that the dormant receivables were not yet collected/written off in CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to correct the misclassification through the following adjusting entry: Dr Buildings 1,926,291.11 Dr Hospitals and health centers 952,795.69 Cr R&M - Buildings and Other Structures 2,411,235.19 Also, to compute for the carrying amount of the existing structures removed and derecognize the amount from the PPE account in accordance with IPSAS 17. Thereafter, ensure that future major repairs are capitalized as part of the Municipality’s PPE and depreciated over the remaining or extended useful life of the related asset in accordance with the aforementioned standards and provisions.
observations.managementAction: The Management informed the Audit Team that the Yellow Market was already demolished and under renovation hence the capitalizable repairs already not applicable. The adjustment for the rehabilitation of the RHU laboratory on the other hand was overlooked and will be corrected in 2024.
observations.reason: The adjustment for rehabilitation of the RHU laboratory was overlooked and will be corrected in 2024. The capitalizable repairs for the Yellow Market are already not applicable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE request authority to donate the properties to the NGOs from the Sangguniang Bayan through a resolution, and thereafter, prepare the corresponding deeds of donation. Also, to submit the deficient acceptance receipts for the donations of farm implements to farmers and certificates of acceptance for donations to barangays for barangay projects. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant review whether there are transactions which cannot be considered as donations and prepare the adjusting entries, if necessary.
observations.managementAction: The Management has submitted the acknowledgment receipt for Pro-poor Electrification. acknowledgement receipts for Pro-poor Electrification were not yet submitted.
observations.reason: The Management submitted the deficient documents except for the submitted acknowledgment receipts for Pro-poor Electrification which were not yet submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE ensure the optimal utilization of the RA 7171 Municipal Share through the proper and timely implementation of programs and projects so that the benefits expected from these programs and projects will be expeditiously delivered to the tobacco farmers. Likewise, require the Municipal Engineer to improve project scheduling to prevent delays in the completion and enable the Municipality to take the appropriate action in case slippages occur.
observations.managementAction: Several projects were completed by the management in the current year.
observations.reason: Further, several projects from CY 2022 were still ongoing or not yet implemented as of December 31, 2023. Several projects funded under RA7171 and RA8240 for CY 2023 were still not implemented hence said funds were not optimally utilized for the benefit of the farmers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management cause the immediate settlement of the disallowance in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009. Also, to strictly adhere to the guidelines on the grant of the PEI.
observations.managementAction: The Management is in the process of securing the necessary documents for the request for the installment payment of the disallowed PEI. However, it lacked the necessary documents as required on COA Resolution No. 2017-021. The audit team required the submission of the lacking documents for the proper processing of the request.
observations.reason: Request for installment has been submitted but it lacked the necessary supporting documents hence, the request was reverted to the Agency.

observations.recommendation:
1.3.3 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the
Municipal Accountant to:

a) Exert effort in identifying and verifying the existence of the
buildings and school buildings recorded under SEF with the
assistance of the Department of Education (DepEd) District
Office where the buildings were entrusted;

b) Discontinue the practice of transferring of assets constructed
and completed from CIP to the appropriate asset account in the
month of December and transfer them upon completion instead;

c) Compute the depreciation of assets completed starting from the
date when it is available for use; and

d) Make the necessary adjusting entries to record the depreciation
of the constructed and completed assets and present them in
their correct carrying amount.

2.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Inventory
Committee and the GSO to ensure that all properties, equipment and
inventories are included in the physical count conducted at year-end for
complete and accurate recording of balances in the FS.

2.8 We further recommend that Management provide an
explanation/justification regarding the non-inclusion of the above-mentioned
farm equipment in the RPCI.

3.2 We recommended that the Local Executive Chief instruct the Municipal
Accountant to:

a) Effect the adjustments proposed in Annex B in the Municipality’s books
of accounts to correct the balance of Other Payables account;

b) Strictly monitor the transactions being credited to the Other Payables
account; and

c) Ensure that all obligations recognized in the books as payables are valid
claims and supported with the necessary supporting documents.

3.3 It was further recommended that the Local Executive Chief require the
Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with DOH for the remittance of the
collected liquidated damages from the DOH-funded project and with DSWD
for the remittance SEA-K Collections.

4.4 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a) Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to establish
strong internal controls in the handling of cash particularly the
collections and remittances from the Collectors to the Municipal
Treasurer, and the collections and deposits made by the Municipal
Treasurer to the Authorized Government Depository Banks (AGDB), to
prevent further incurrence of unrecorded collections and/or over-deposit
of collections, which resulted to abnormal/negative balances of the Cash
Local Treasury account;

b) Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to conduct
a thorough investigation to identify the root cause of the discrepancies
between the financial statements and the cashbook after which take
appropriate action to correct the discrepancies in accordance with the
applicable accounting standards and guidelines;

c) Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to implement
adequate monitoring and reconciliation procedures for all accounts,
including the Cash-Local Treasury account and Cash in Bank-LCCA, to
prevent future discrepancies and ensure the accuracy of financial reports;

d) Direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately effect adjustment in the
books of these long outstanding reconciling items for a fair presentation
of cash balances;

e) Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that supporting documents
of items requiring recording/adjustment in the books are secured, book
reconciling items are recorded immediately in the ensuing month, and
respective banks are immediately informed of any bank errors for their
immediate action; and

f) Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure compliance with Section 27
of IPSAS 4 and present the translated balance of Cash in Bank - Foreign
Currency, Savings Account based on the closing rate at each reporting
date.

5.10 We recommended the following:

a) The Municipal Treasurer:

i. send a written notice to the payees to inform them of the checks that
have become stale;

ii. issue new checks to payees who responded to the notice and upon
submission of the stale checks; and

iii. henceforth adhere to the provision of Section 59 of NGAS Volume I
to send a written notice to the payee at least one month before a check
becomes stale;

b) The Municipal Accountant shall make the necessary adjusting entries to
properly record the cancellation and replacement of stale checks in
accordance with Section 59 of NGAS Volume I and Section 97 of PD 1445
as follows:

b.1.1 For stale checks over 6 months but not over 2 years:
Account Title Account Debit Credit
Code
Check Cash in Bank -Local Currency, Current Account 101 xxx
cancellation Accounts Payable 401 xxx
To recognize the cancellation of stale/voided/spoiled commercial
checks
Replacement Accounts Payable 401 xxx
Cash in Bank -Local Currency, 101 xxx
Current Account
To record replacement of cancelled stale check.

b.1.2 For stale checks over 2 years:
Account Title Account Debit Credit
Code
Cash in Bank -Local Currency, Current Account 101 xxx
Prior Years’ Adjustment 533 xxx
To recognize the cancellation of stale/voided/spoiled commercial checks

6.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant to:

a) Discontinue the use of GAM for LGUs and revert back to implementing
the accounting policies and procedures based on NGAS;

b) Henceforth, set-up RPT and SET Receivable according to the pro-forma
entries prescribed by NGAS and based on the duly certified list of
taxpayers prepared by the Municipal Treasurer at the beginning of the year
containing the amount due and collectible for the year for each taxpayer;

c) Reconcile the balances of RPT/SET Receivable with the list of delinquent
taxpayers prepared and certified by the Municipal Treasurer to ensure
accuracy in the reported account balances as at year-end;

d) Adjust the RPT/SET Receivable and Deferred RPT/SET accounts to
reflect their correct balances which is the uncollected RPT/SET including
the share of other LGUs; and

e) Adjust the RPT-Basic/SET income to reflect the total amount of collected
Municipal share

6.9 It is also recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Treasurer to:

a) Prepare a duly certified list at the beginning of the year containing the
name of taxpayers and the corresponding amount due and collectible for
the year traced from the individual Real Property Tax Account Register of the
taxpayers. This report shall be the basis of the Municipal Accountant in the
establishment of RPT and SET Receivable at the beginning of the year in
order to avoid over/understatement of the receivable and deferred income
accounts;

b) Prepare a certified list of delinquent taxpayers as of the reporting date
which shall be reconciled with the accounting records. This is to ensure
that the reported balances of RPT and SET Receivable as at the reporting
date is complete and accurate.

7.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the
Municipal Accountant to exert more effort, follow-up on the debtors and
serve corresponding demand letters, and conduct a thorough investigation on
the Other Receivables account in identifying details of the dormant receivables
to establish the claim of the Municipality. Also, to assess the recoverability of
the long outstanding receivables and make provision for uncollectible
receivables, as necessary. Should there be no other remedies for collection, we
recommend the LCE to request for authority to write-off dormant receivables
pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005.

8.10 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to:

a) Discontinue the practice of setting up or recognizing payables at year-end
without complete and valid documents supporting the claims;

b) Conduct a thorough review of the Accounts Payable account as of year-end
and locate the supporting documents of the unsupported claims, in
coordination with the concerned departments, to determine if the claims
are valid or not; and

c) Adjust the Accounts Payable account to reflect its correct balance.

8.11 We further recommended the termination of the uncompleted contracts in
accordance with the procedures provided in Annex I of the 2016 Revised IRR
of RA No. 9164.

9.8 We recommend that the Local Executive Chief ensure the optimal utilization
of the RA Nos. 7171 and 8240 Municipal Share through the proper and
timely implementation of programs and projects so that the benefits expected
from these programs and projects will be expeditiously delivered to the
tobacco farmers. Likewise, require the Municipal Engineer to improve
project scheduling to prevent delays in the completion and enable the
Municipality to take the appropriate action in case slippages occur.

10.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to:

a) Stop the remittance of Government’s Share in Pag-IBIG Fund
Contributions in excess of the prescribed rate;

b) Require officials and employees who wish to increase their share in the
Pag-IBIG Fund contributions to fill out a Request To Upgrade
Membership Savings (MS) Form downloadable from the Pag-IBIG
website;

c) Request Pag-IBIG Fund to consider the excess amount remitted as
advance payment and reclassify the excess Government’s Share
remitted including those from prior years to Other Prepayments
account;

d) Strictly adhere to the laws/regulations on Pag-IBIG Fund Contributions
particularly the provisions of RA No. 9679 and Pag-IBIG Fund Circular No.
460 which took effect in February 2024 increasing the maximum monthly
contribution of employees and employers to (₱200.00); and

e) Strengthen the internal controls on remittances of statutory obligations
to prevent the recurrence of this deficiency.

11.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the General
Services Officer/Supply Officer to ensure that all disbursement transactions are
supported with valid, complete and appropriate documentary requirements in
accordance with PD 1445 and pertinent BIR and COA Circulars.

11.8 We also recommend that Management instruct the Inspection Committee to
require sales invoices or official receipts on all purchases from suppliers
instead of a collection receipt.

12.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the BAC to ensure
compliance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184
particularly on alternative modes of procurement.

13.8 We recommended that the Municipality consider the advantages of utilizing
a Framework Agreement, as outlined in Government Procurement Policy Board
(GPPB) Resolution No. 27-2019.

14.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Engineer to exercise due diligence in the preparation of the Quarterly Report on
Government PPAs and ensure that all projects whether on-going or
completed are properly included and accurately reported.

15.11 We recommended that the Management:

a) In coordination with the Sangguniang Bayan establish guidelines for the
grant of communication allowances to its officials and employees through
the passage of an SB Resolution;

b) Adopt and implement control measures by limiting the grant to not lower
than the Department Head (Division Chief) rank, limit the amount
granted to a conscionable rate and any excess amounts shall be
shouldered by the user;

c) Review the amounts granted for communication allowance in relation to
the available landlines/mobile phones and internet connection and
consider its reduction;

d) Consider the procurement of load cards in bulk by the GSO monthly or
quarterly, following the required procurement procedures, and
distribute the same in accordance with the established guidelines; and

e) Discontinue the charging of Communication Allowances to Telephone
Expenses account and record it under Other Bonus and Allowances
account subject to withholding tax if they opt to continue receiving the
communication allowances in cash.

16.7 We recommended that the Management:

a) Stop the practice of issuing mobile phones in excess of one unit to the
officers and employees of the Municipality and require the return of the
phones initially issued to the General Services Officer.

b) Limit the issuance of cellular phones to qualified officials and employees
which should not be lower than the Division Chief rank. In case there is a
necessity for the issuance of phones to an employee below the Division Chief
rank, this must be duly justified as to its necessity in the execution of his/her
duties and responsibilities

c) Establish controls in the procurement of machineries and equipment such as
evaluation of the condition of the previously purchased units and conduct
cost-benefit analysis to assess whether they need replacement to avoid
incurrence of excessive expenditures.

17.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, in coordination with the
Sangguniang Bayan, cause the amendment of SB Ordinance No. 2015-08 and
establish clear-cut procedural guidelines for all types of donations to be
granted by the Municipal Government. Said guidelines should include
provisions for the following, among others:

a) The eligibility of the beneficiaries for the grant;

b) The criteria in determining the amount of donation to be given in every
case and the designated person/s who shall be responsible to determine
the same;

c) The frequency of availment of donations; and

d) The complete list of documents to be required for each type of donation.

17.7 We further recommended the incorporation of the medical assistance
granted to indigents for the payment of their laboratory test expenses in the
amended SB Ordinance No. 2015-08 for uniformity of guidelines on the
donations granted to individuals.

18.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC to submit
the deficient APP, and ABC of the identified procurement transactions and
to strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and the 2016
Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in order to ensure that the procurement
transactions are properly supported with complete documentation and
preserve the integrity and effectiveness of the procurement process.

We recommended that the LCE require the GSO, Inspectorate Committee, and Requisitioning Office/Department implement measures to ensure that source documents, particularly PR, PO and AIR, are properly filled out and contain all required information in accordance with the GAM for LGUs to improve the efficiency and transparency of its procurement process. Also, require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all the aforementioned documents are complete and duly filled out to reduce the risk of errors or discrepancies in its financial records, hence accuracy and reliability of its financial statements are ensured.
observations.managementAction: The Management has complied with the recommendations. Verified during the post-audit of transactions and noted that the PRs, POs and AIRs were properly and completely filled out.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE oblige the Municipal Treasurer to require the supplier/s submission of the Official Receipts for the transactions identified and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant after which, the latter shall submit the same to the Audit Team. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all disbursement transactions are supported with valid, complete and appropriate documentary requirements in accordance with PD 1445 and pertinent COA circulars.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted the deficient documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the General Services Officer/Supply Officer to require the supplier to submit the deficient DRs for the transactions identified. Also, instruct the Inspection Committee to always require the DRs from the supplier upon actual delivery of the procured items and reconcile the same with Purchase Order which will be the basis in the preparation of the Acceptance and Inspection Report (AIR). Moreover, require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all disbursement transactions are supported with valid, complete and appropriate documentary requirements in accordance with PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001, thus the validity and propriety of the transactions could be ascertained.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the deficient DRs for the transactions identified. Also noted during post audit of Disbursement Vouchers that the procurement of goods was properly supported with DRs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management submit the deficient PPMP, APP, and ABC of the identified procurement transactions and to strictly adhere to the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 in order to preserve the integrity and effectiveness of the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: Management has submitted the deficient PPMP, APP, and ABC of the identified procurement transactions and complied with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Assessor to prioritize obtaining the Transfer Certificates of Title for the parcels of land in accordance with Section 39(2) of Presidential Decree (PD) 1445 to serve as proof of absolute ownership over these properties.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor’s Office notified the audit team that the titling process is on-going. Two of the lots which were previously for conveyance were donated to PNP and BJMP.
observations.reason: Per verification, no other lots were titled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Executive Chief ensure the optimal utilization of the RA 7171 Municipal Share through the proper and timely implementation of programs and projects so that the benefits expected from these programs and projects will be expeditiously delivered to the tobacco farmers. Likewise, require the Municipal Engineer to improve project scheduling to prevent delays in completion and enable the Municipality to take the appropriate action in case slippages occur.
observations.managementAction: The projects were completed by the management in the current year.
observations.reason: The RA7171 fund was not optimally utilized. This is reiterated in Finding No. 9 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management cause the immediate settlement of the disallowance in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009. Also, to strictly adhere on the guidelines on the grant of the PEI.
observations.managementAction: The Management is in the process of collecting the disallowance from the accountable officers.
observations.reason: The settlement of the audit disallowance is in process.

observations.recommendation:

Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to:
1.6.1 conduct a thorough investigation to identify the root cause of the
discrepancies between the financial statements and the cashbook
after which take appropriate action to correct the discrepancies in
accordance with the applicable accounting standards and
guidelines; and
1.6.2 implement adequate monitoring and reconciliation procedures for
all accounts, including the Cash Local Treasury account, to
prevent future discrepancies and ensure the accuracy of financial
reports.

2.6 We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to
exert more effort, follow-up on the debtors and serve corresponding demand
letters, and conduct a thorough investigation on the Other Receivables
account in identifying details of the dormant receivables to establish the
claim of the Municipality. Also, to assess the recoverability of the long
outstanding receivables and make provision for uncollectible receivables,
as necessary.

3.10 We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to
correct the misclassification through the following adjusting entry:
Dr Buildings 1,926,291.11
Dr Hospitals and health centers 952,795.69
Cr R&M - Buildings and Other Structures 2,411,235.19

Also, to compute for the carrying amount of the existing structures
removed and derecognize the amount from the PPE account in
accordance with IPSAS 17.

Thereafter, ensure that future major repairs are capitalized as part of the
Municipality’s PPE and depreciated over the remaining or extended

We recommended that Management require the Appraisal Committee to facilitate and finalize the appraisal of the 16 parcels of land included in the RPCPPE. Further, require the Municipal Accountant to recognize the results of the appraisal as an adjustment to the opening balance of accumulated surpluses or deficits for the period they were recognized as instructed in Paragraph 97 of IPSAS 17, thus the affected accounts will be fairly presented in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Verified the appraisal of the land. Management has appraised the parcels of land and recorded the values of the land in the books of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Reviewed the adjusting entries posted for the appraisal. Found in order.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management file a request to the Source Agencies for authority to use the unexpended fund transfers, if the disposition thereof has not been provided in the Memorandum of Agreement, Trust Memorandum or Memorandum of Understanding, as provided for under Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001. We further recommended that Management remit the unexpended fund transfer balances to the Source Agencies, if the authority for the use of the unexpended fund transfers was not approved. Furthermore, monitor the fund transfer utilization and return the unexpended balances upon completion of the projects in accordance with Section 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94-013.
observations.managementAction: During the post-audit of transactions, we have found that the Management has returned the fund transfers to the source agencies.
observations.reason: Found in order.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal GSO to submit the deficient certified and duly accomplished lists of recipients and to ensure that all issuances of procured inventory and property for distribution are supported with the required lists of recipients in accordance with Section 4, item (6) of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Management has submitted the deficient documents.
observations.reason: Verified the documents. Found in order.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Bids and Awards Committee, BAC Secretariat and TWG Members of the Municipality, at all times, be guided with the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 in the procurement of goods and services, infrastructure projects and consulting services. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to submit the deficient documents and ensure that all supporting documents for all types of transactions are complete and fully substantiated.
observations.managementAction: Management has submitted the deficient documents.
observations.reason: Verified the Bids submitted documents. Found in order.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Executive Chief ensure the utilization of the 20% Development Fund to help achieve desirable socio-economic and environmental outcomes, for the projects under the municipality and its intended beneficiaries. Likewise, require the Municipal Engineer to continuously monitor the status of on-going projects and ensure the completion of the same within the target date.
observations.managementAction: The projects were implemented in the ensuing year.
observations.reason: In the current year, 20% Development Fund was optimally utilized with utilization rate of 78%.

observations.recommendation:
For the proper management and monitoring of the Due from NGOs/POs, Advances to Officers and Employees and Other Receivables accounts, we recommended that the Management:

Require concerned employees to intensify collection of the long outstanding receivables and send demand letter, if necessary.
observations.managementAction: Management has intensified collection through communication with the existing debtors. However, no favorable replies were received.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management file a request to the Source Agencies for authority to use the unexpended fund transfers, if the disposition thereof has not been provided in the Memorandum of Agreement, Trust Memorandum or Memorandum of Understanding, as provided for under Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to remit a portion of the unexpended balances. As at December 31, 2021, the outstanding balance of the unexpended transfers amounted to ₱526,478.19. Management continues to communicate with Source Agencies for these transfer balances.
observations.reason: Unexpended transfers remained unreturned totaling ₱526,478.19. Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Assessor to prioritize obtaining the Original Certificates of Title of the 21 parcels of land and the Transfer Certificates of Title for the parcels of land acquired by the Municipality to serve as proof of absolute ownership over these properties.
observations.managementAction: Management is still on the process of securing Certificates of Title for the parcels of land. As at December 31, 2021, no titles were submitted to the audit team.
observations.reason: As at December 31, 2021, no titles were submitted to the audit team. Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Appraisal Committee to facilitate and finalize the appraisal of the 16 parcels of land included in the RPCPPE. Further, require the Municipal Accountant to recognize the results of the appraisal as an adjustment to the opening balance of accumulated surpluses or deficits for the period they were recognized as instructed in Paragraph 97 of IPSAS Volume II No. 17, thus the affected accounts will be fairly presented in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management plans to appraise the parcels of land based on the Assessed Values indicated in their corresponding Tax Declarations. Recording of the cost of these properties are yet to be made.
observations.reason: The balances of the parcels of land are not yet reflected in the financial statements as at December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant continue to strictly adhere to the documentation of all disbursement vouchers provided in COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 in compliance with Section 6 of PD 1445 before effecting payment to avoid suspensions and disallowances.
observations.managementAction: Management received COA Commission Proper Decision No. 2021-533 dated December 23, 2021 affirming ND Nos. 2015-001-101-(2014) and 2015-002-101-(2014) on the excess payment of PEI to the officials and employees of the municipality for CY 2014.

observations.managementAction: For current accounts, monitoring and analysis are done regularly by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: Furthermore, we recommended that the Municipal Government monitor the fund transfer utilization and return the unexpended balances upon completion of the projects in accordance with Item 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94-013 or the terms in the Memorandum of Agreement/Project implementation guidelines.
observations.managementAction: The projects funded by fund transfers, which were implemented in 2020, were paid late in the year, hence, no refund was made yet.
observations.reason: Verified through inquiry and review of submitted reports that transfers in the current years are monitored regularly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant facilitate the immediate submission of the lacking documents. We recommended further that the Office of the Municipal Accountant henceforth prepare a checklist of documentary requirements prescribed by COA Circular No. 2012-001 and attach the same to the disbursement vouchers so that all concerned officials and employees are reminded of the required documents and able to check which document is not yet attached,  thus ensuring all transactions are duly supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 6 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and to avoid suspension of transaction in post-audit.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted the noted deficiencies in the documentary requirements found in audit.
observations.reason: Verified the submitted documentary requirements noted, in order.

observations.reason: Verified that the delays did not cause negative slippage and excess in the maximum procurement period as prescribed by RA 9184.

observations.recommendation:
For proper monitoring of Due to NGAs account, we recommended the following procedures:

Management ensure the return of unexpended balances of projects funded by NGAs returned to the respective NGAs to enable them to use these funds for other programs/projects/ activities of the agencies;

The Municipal Accountant conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the existence of fund transfer under the Due to NGAs account, reconcile the unliquidated fund transfer between the source and implementing agencies, prepare the necessary adjusting entries for the reconciling items noted, and require liquidation of balances

If not returned, Management ask authority to use these balances for another project.
observations.managementAction: The unexpended balances were liquidated and returned to the respective NGAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, BAC and BAC Secretariat ensure that all small value procurements of the Municipality comply with the documentary requirements for alternative methods of procurement as prescribed by Annex “H”, Appendix A of 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 before payment.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with our recommendations on the documentary requirements for small-value procurements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure the immediate implementation and timely completion of funded projects so that constituents could derive the benefits from these projects determined by them as priorities.
observations.managementAction: These projects were implemented in 2018 in accordance with our recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board oversee the effective implementation projects/projects/ activities under the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan such as the Sanitary Landfill Development and adhere to the provisions of RA 9003 in order to ensure the protection of public health and environment.
observations.managementAction: In 2018, current and continuing appropriation for Sanitary Landfill Development were utilized in accordance with our recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Executive Chief ensure that the 20% Development Fund is optimally utilized to help achieve desirable socio-economic and environmental outcomes for the municipality and its intended beneficiaries.  

We also encourage that Management appropriate the fund for allowable development projects that are provided under DILG-DBM JMC NO. 2017-01 dated February 22, 2017.
observations.managementAction: In 2018, 95.69% of the total current and continuing appropriations of 20% Development Fund were utilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management conduct an appraisal of the 16 pieces of land included in the RPCPPE to determine their fair value which will be recognized in the books by the Municipal Accountant as an adjustment to the opening balance of accumulated surpluses or deficits for the period they were recognized as instructed in Paragraph 97 of PPSAS Vol. II No. 17.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to obtain Transfer Certificates of Title of all parcels of land acquired by the Municipality to serve as proof of absolute ownership over these properties.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require physical inventory taking of all inventories reflected in the financial statements to ascertain their accuracy and existence for a fair presentation.

For the proper management and monitoring of the Due from NGOs/POs, Advances to Officers and Employees and Other Receivables accounts, we recommended the following:
The Local Chief Executive and Municipal Accountant require concerned employees to intensify collection of the long outstanding receivables and send demand letter, if necessary.
The Local Chief Executive file a request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts without any possibility of collection in accordance with the provisions of COA Cir. No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant and concerned officials, liquidate, monitor and analyze on a regular basis all receivable accounts to ensure collection when they become due and demandable in accordance with the provisions of COA Cir. No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain special accounts under the GF for all economic enterprises of the Municipality so that Management could monitor their operational efficiency and use the data derived from the financial statements as basis in decision making for the improvement of such.

We recommended that the MDRRMO submit monthly Report on Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund on time and the LDRRMFIP be prepared and in accordance with COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the General Service Officer and all concerned officials see to it that all Purchase Orders for procurement of goods contain all the necessary information as prescribed under Section B.1 of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant instruct the users of government vehicles to fill-up all the required information on the driver’s trip tickets and require the attachment of accomplishment reports to payment of gasoline used by construction and heavy equipment vehicles.

We recommended that the Municipal Government and the Municipal School Board adhere to the programs, projects and activities provided in DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 in the appropriation, allocation and disbursement of Special Education Fund. Also, refrain from charging expenses which are not within the bounds and regulations on SEF so that funds would be utilized exclusively for the purpose/s it was created.

For proper monitoring of Due to NGAs account, we recommended the following procedures:
Management ensure the return of unexpended balances of projects funded by NGAs are returned to the respective NGAs to enable them to use these funds for other programs/projects/activities of the agencies;
the Municipal Accountant conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the existence of fund transfers under the Due to NGAs account, reconcile the unliquidated fund transfers between the source and implementing agencies, prepare the necessary adjusting entries for the reconciling items noted, and require liquidation of balances.
If not returned, Management ask authority to use these balances for another project.

We recommended that the Municipal Council for the Protection of Children see it that the appropriation for the Protection of Children should be equal to or more than one percent (1%) of the Internal Revenue Allotment in compliance with Section 15 of Republic Act 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012 to ensure that all plans for children are provided with funds and implemented.

We recommended that the BAC, BAC Secretariat and TWG Members of the Municipality, at all times, be guided by the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 in the procurement of goods and services, infrastructure projects and consulting services under the RA 7171 fund in order to ensure propriety, validity and legality of the procurement.

We recommended the following measures/procedures to correct the above deficiencies to ensure the validity of transactions:
That the OIC-Municipal Accountant see it that all supporting documents for all types of transactions are complete.
That the BAC, thru the BAC Secretariat see it that, before submitting the contracts for approval to the HOPE, the construction safety and health programs are approved by DOLE.
That the BAC, BAC Secretariat and TWG Members of the Municipality, at all times, be guided with the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 in the procurement of goods and services, infrastructure projects and consulting services.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, BAC and BAC Secretariat ensure that all small value procurements of the Municipality comply with the documentary requirements for alternative methods of procurement as prescribed by Annex “H”, Appendix A of 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 before payment.

We recommended that Management ensure the immediate implementation and timely completion of funded projects so that constituents could derive the benefits from these projects determined by them as priorities.

We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board  oversee the effective implementation of the programs/projects/activities under the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan such as the Sanitary Landfill Development and adhere to the provisions of RA 9003 in order to ensure the protection of public health and environment.

We recommended that the Local Executive Chief ensure that the 20% Development Fund is optimally utilized to help achieve desirable socio-economic and environmental outcomes for the municipality and its intended beneficiaries.
We also encourage that Management appropriate the fund for allowable development projects that are provided under DILG-DBM JMC NO. 2017-01 dated February 22, 2017.
We recommended that the GAD Focal Person submit the GPB bearing the approval of the PCW and corresponding Accomplishment Report within the required time to facilitate immediate review of the same.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly adhere in the documentation of all disbursement vouchers provided in COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 in compliance with Section 6 of PD 1445 before effecting payment to avoid suspensions and disallowances.
